The Court noted that there were several issues raised in the Probate Examiner's notes of March 15, 2019, that they have not been addressed and that the matter has been continued several times. Mr. Dewys requested a four week continuance stating that there was some confusion, that he believes the amended petition addresses the Examiner's concerns and that some concerns lingered. Mr. Dewys also stated that he is meeting with Ms. Stewart next week to obtain a waiver to address the notice issue. The Amended Petition for Final Distribution, etc. is continued to June 3, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Chris DeWys for Linda Johnson (Administrator)

Mr. DeWys apologized for missing the previous hearing date, stated he has been unable to reach his client, and is waiting for receipts from Ms. Stewart for her claim for funeral expenses. The Court noted that Mr. DeWys could have called if he had a scheduling conflict, and expressed his concern that Mr. DeWys is not in contact with his client. An Order to Appear will be issued to the Administrator, Linda Johnson, to appear on July 10, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why she failed to appear and why the Petition for Final Distribution or Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration has not been filed. The Court will consider on that date, whether it is necessary to suspend or remove Ms. Johnson as Administrator. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing Re Filing Receipts For Deposits Into Blocked Acccounts.
Off Calendar Feb-06-2018 Continued To Feb-27-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Chris Dewys for petitioner Linda Johnson; Pamela Stewart in pro per.

Mr. Dewys reported that two of the three banks where blocked accounts were to be set up now refused to open accounts in the names of the joint administrators. Only Wells Fargo Bank is now willing to open such an account at this time, but Bank of America have adopted a policy against opening such joint accounts. He suggested the best way to proceed would be for Pamela Stewart to resign as joint administrator, which would then permit the remaining administrator to open the accounts and deposit the checks. Ms. Stewart stated that she was willing to resign, provided she is provided with the information confirming the amount of the deposits and what is done with the money. Mr. Dewys stated that all liens on the property have been paid off; the balance of proceeds is $568,000, there are two small creditors' claims remaining each of which is under $2000. The Court accepts the resignation of Pamela Stewart, and directs Mr. Dewys to submit a revised order and the filing of a petition for final distribution. Mr. Deqys said he should be able to get that petition on file in three weeks. The status hearing is set for February 27, 2018, at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung. (Not reported)

Petition To Remove Pamela Stewart As Co Administrator From The Estate Of Virginia Stewart
Off Calendar Dec-19-2017 Continued To Jan-16-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Chris Dewys for Linda Johnson (Petitioner/Co-Administrator).
Pamela Stewart in Propria Persona.

Mr. Dewys explained the difficulty with the Banks in opening the blocked accounts and the bank's requirement regarding two signatures and the parties being present when signing, expressed that he has no confidence in being able to rely on Ms. Stewart continuing her responsibilities to administer the estate and stated that each Beneficiary's distribution is $280,000.00. The Court raised the issue of the amendment not having been filed in advance enough of today's hearing and Ms. Stewart resigning as Administrator which would streamline getting the money to her. Ms. Stewart stated that she wants to get her money and that she has no problem with being where she needs to be when needed. The Amended Petition to Remove Pamela Stewart as Co-Administrator is continued to January 16, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for sufficient notice time for the amended petition to be considered. The Court encourages Ms. Stewart to seek an Attorney and be available to get things signed.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Petition To Remove Pamela Stewart As Co Administrator From The Estate Of Virginia Stewart
Off Calendar Nov-14-2017 Continued To Dec-19-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Chirs Dewys for Linda Johnson (Co-Administrator).
Pamela Stewart in Propria Persona.

Mr. Dewys stated that he has a report from the Server indicating that Ms. Stewart has been evicted from the property, that he mailed documents to the only other address he knows of and that he has heard nothing. The Court raised the issue of actual service of a citation. Mr. Dewys stated that the Clerk would not issue a citation. The Court noted that it was not part of the prayer. Mr. Dewys stated that he can not deposit the funds or do an inventory without the signatures of both parties. The Petition to Remove Pamela Stewart as Co-Administrator is continued to December 19, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for an amended petition to address the above issue.

Pamela Stewart checked in with the Clerk two hours after the matter had been called. The Court made an effort to contact Mr. Dewys for return to Court, but was unsuccessful. The Court advised Ms. Stewart that there are some papers she needs to sign that will get money from the sale into the Trust account and get it distributed to her and Ms. Johnson. The Court also informed Ms. Stewart that a petition to remove her has been filed, that it has been available for several months, and that she needs to resign or go over to Mr. Dewys' office. Ms. Stewart provided a P.O. Box address and phone number, and stated that she does not have a street address. The Court ordered Ms. Stewart to be present at the December 19, 2017 hearing.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing A Final Inventory & Appraisal And Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-13-2016 Continued To Oct-11-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christopher Dewys for co-administrator Linda Johnson; co-administrator Pamela Stewart, in pro per.

The Court asked Ms. Stewart if she was willing to cooperate so the administration of the estate could proceed. Ms. Stewart stated that she was willing to do whatever it takes to get the estate settled. The Court asked Mr. Dewys what was needed from Ms. Stewart. Mr. Dewys said that he needed an itemization of all the assets in order to complete the inventory. Ms. Stewart agreed to provide a list of the assets. Mr Dewys stated that he also needed to be in communication with the bank. Ms Stewart stated her willingness to have Mr. Dewys in communication with the bank provided she was included in the communication. The Court suggested that the parties produce a writing to the bank that allows three-way conversations which permit both co-administrators to be involved. Mr. Dewys will prepare an appropriate order. The matter was continued to October 11, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar May-31-2016 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Chris Dewys for (co-administrator) Linda Johnson.

The Court asked if Ms. Stewart has been heard from. Mr. Dewys stated that he has not heard from her, that they have been able to stave off foreclosure of the property several times, that the mortgage holder will not talk to him without authorization from Ms. Stewart, that he is not sure if she is still living in the property, that he continues to send correspondence there, that the locks have been changed, that they have no access to the property, that Ms. Stewart staves off the foreclosure sales at the last minute, that his client has agreed to pay 1/2 of the mortgage and is sending Ms. Stewart $150.00 per month and that their hands are tied regarding any additional assets. The Court stated that we are here on a status of filing an Inventory & Appraisal and that a partial has been filed. Mr. Dewys stated that the only uncertainty is the $5000.00 in assets and what is in the house. The Court stated that this status could go off calendar. Mr. Dewys stated that Ms. Stewart has agreed to a potential realtor to sell the house and that if there is no movement in 45 days he will file a petition to remove her. The Court took the status of filing an Inventory & Appraisal off calendar and set a status date for filing of a final Inventory & Accounting and a Petition for Final Distribution for August 2, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel - Civil
Off Calendar Jan-21-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for (co-administrator) Pamela Stewart. Chris Dewys for (co-administrator) Linda Johnson. Janet Boessenecker for the San Francisco Public Administrator.

The Court stated that this is Ms. Ng's motion to be relieved as counsel, that she is unable to get the cooperation of her client, confirmed that there have been no objections and asked Mr. Dewys if his client will be able to continue. Mr. Dewys stated that he has told her what is expected and that she states she is ready to go forward. The Court stated that its information states that Ms. Johnson is represented by Trang La. Mr. Dewys stated that he has spoken with Trang La and that a substitution of attorney is in the works. Ms. Ng reported that she has not been in touch with her client and that she had to hire a process server to serve the motion. Mr. Dewys stated that Ms. Stewart and Ms. Johnson are not in touch and that there is a riff between them. The Court asked if she will be able to deal with this and stated that if Mr. Dewys and his client can carry this through the Public Administrator will not be needed. Ms. Boessenecker stated that she spoke with Mr. Dewys this week, that the administrators are the two parties with everything to win or lose, that she suggests removing Ms. Stewart and appointing Ms. Johnson as sole administrator and then to get Ms. Stewart out of the house and get it sold. The Court asked if the foreclosure has actually begun. Ms. Ng stated that she has received notices from the mortgage company and that February 27, 2016 will be the end of the 3 month period. The Court granted the motion to be relieved as counsel (subject to review of the order), stating that its concerns have been alleviated by Mr. Dewys representing Ms. Johnson, that we do not have an inventory & appraisal or petition for final distribution and set a status of March 15, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of the inventory and appraisal.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Lee. (Not Reported)
